title: Extract default_config_json helper from ensure_config (pure refactor)
action: Extract the default-config JSON construction currently inlined inside `ensure_config` (src/config/mod.rs, the `format!` block building bind_addr/herdr_session/allowed_roots/poll_interval_ms/herdr_protocol/static_dir/agent_presets) into a standalone function `pub(crate) fn default_config_json(root: &std::path::Path) -> String`, and have `ensure_config` call this new function instead of inlining the format! string. This is a PURE REFACTOR -- the emitted JSON text must be byte-for-byte identical to what ensure_config produced before, for the same root argument. This satisfies D5: the merge feature's source-of-truth for 'new version default config' is this exact function, reused, never a separate template. Add a unit test named exactly `default_config_json_produces_expected_keys` asserting the function's output parses as valid JSON and contains every top-level key ensure_config's default previously had. Do not change Config, ConfigError, or any other function's behavior.

truths: default_config_json's output is valid JSON containing bind_addr, herdr_session, allowed_roots, poll_interval_ms, herdr_protocol, static_dir, and agent_presets

Every pre-existing config module test still passes unchanged, in particular ensure_config_creates_a_working_default_then_reloads and ensure_config_seeds_default_agent_presets -- no behavior change from the refactor

artifacts.substantive: default_config_json extracted and called by ensure_config; new test default_config_json_produces_expected_keys added; no TODO stubs

key_links: ensure_config calls default_config_json instead of inlining the format! string
prohibitions: No change to the JSON content/shape produced for a given root argument
No changes to the Config struct or ConfigError

verify: grep -q 'fn default_config_json' src/config/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn default_config_json_produces_expected_keys' src/config/mod.rs && cargo test --quiet config

trace.outcome: Extracted default_config_json(root) from ensure_config; ensure_config now calls it. JSON output byte-identical for a given root (D5). Added default_config_json_produces_expected_keys; all config tests green.
trace.verify_output: both greps matched; cargo test config: 64 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored (incl. ensure_config_creates_a_working_default_then_reloads, ensure_config_seeds_default_agent_presets, default_config_json_produces_expected_keys)
trace.verify_command: grep -q 'fn default_config_json' src/config/mod.rs && grep -q 'fn default_config_json_produces_expected_keys' src/config/mod.rs && cargo test --quiet config
